body_txt: Nurarihyon (滑瓢) are Yōkai that would look like humans if only their heads didn't protrude so far behind them. Sometimes it's a spherical head, other times the protrusion consists of multiple bumps. Nurarihyon wear kessa (Buddhist robes) and carry one or even two swords with them to signal their importance. They're always male and aristocratic, and in the modern day, unbasedly viewed as commanders of other youkai. Notably, they lead other spirits during the Night Parade of One Hundred Demons . In that regard, theirs is comparable to the status assigned to vampires in Western works. Perhaps due to this perception, Nurarihyon are considered to be impossible to catch. "Nurari" means "slippery" and "hyon" combines the meanings of "floating on the surface" and "(dried) gourd". Underlying this etymology is its knack for invading human households to drink their tea, eat their food, and, generally get up to mischief by tricking or forcing his hosts into catering to his whims. Only after Nurarihyon has left would they realize he wasn't their head of family but a stranger. Sub-Trope of Rubber-Forehead Aliens and One-Gender Race . Might overlap with Monster Lord , My Brain Is Big , Tea Is Classy , and Evil Old Folks . Compare Maou the Demon King .
